messages.content: Your latest A1C results are in. They look good, but let's discuss your morning fasting levels at our next visit.
I've reviewed your glucose logs. Please continue the current insulin dosage for another week.

careEvents.title: Initial Diabetes Diagnosis and Management Plan
careEvents.description: First office visit where Type 2 Diabetes was diagnosed based on fasting glucose level of 156 mg/dL and HbA1c of 7.8%. Patient counseled on disease process, lifestyle modifications, and initiated on Metformin 500mg twice daily. Referral provided to diabetes educator for further education and nutrition counseling.

upcomingAppointments.reason: 3-month diabetes management review and medication adjustment
Dietary planning and glucose monitoring education

careEvents.title: Annual Wellness Visit
careEvents.description: First annual physical examination. Patient reports feeling well with no acute complaints. Vital signs normal. Physical examination unremarkable. Patient counseled on preventive health measures and health maintenance.

careEvents.title: Emergency Department Visit - Ankle Fracture
careEvents.description: Patient presented to urgent care with acute right ankle fracture sustained during recreational soccer match. X-ray imaging confirmed non-displaced fracture of right lateral malleolus. Ankle immobilized with fiber glass cast. Pain managed with ibuprofen. Follow-up orthopedic evaluation scheduled.

upcomingAppointments.reason: 6-week post-fracture evaluation and cast removal assessment

careEvents.title: Initial OB Intake Visit
careEvents.description: First prenatal visit. Patient confirmed pregnancy at 10 weeks gestation. Vital signs stable, no complications. Ultrasound confirmed viable intrauterine pregnancy with appropriate dating. Patient educated on prenatal care, nutrition, and activity modifications. Prescribed prenatal vitamins.

upcomingAppointments.reason: Second trimester prenatal visit and anatomy ultrasound

careEvents.title: Hypertension Management Follow-up
careEvents.description: Initial comprehensive hypertension assessment with baseline BP readings and medication regimen review. Patient reported well on current therapy.

careEvents.title: Lipid Panel and Metabolic Screening
careEvents.description: Fasting labs ordered: Total cholesterol, LDL, HDL, triglycerides, glucose, creatinine, electrolytes. All results within acceptable ranges.

careEvents.title: Cardiology Consultation
careEvents.description: Referred to cardiology for hypertension optimization and coronary risk assessment. EKG performed, results normal. Medications confirmed appropriate.

careEvents.title: Carotid Ultrasound Screening
careEvents.description: Non-invasive vascular imaging to assess for atherosclerotic disease. No significant stenosis detected.

careEvents.title: Hypertension and Metabolic Panel Follow-up
careEvents.description: Repeat labs: BP control stable, lipids improved on current atorvastatin dose, glucose well-managed on metformin.

careEvents.title: Primary Care Hypertension Check-in
careEvents.description: 6-month follow-up visit. Blood pressure readings 128/82 and 130/84 mmHg. Patient adherent to medications and lifestyle modifications. Discussed diet, exercise, and salt reduction.

careEvents.title: Hypertension and Heart Health Education Session
careEvents.description: Participated in group education session covering DASH diet, stress management, home BP monitoring techniques, and medication adherence strategies.

careEvents.title: Annual Hypertension Management Review
careEvents.description: Scheduled annual visit with primary care physician for comprehensive hypertension reassessment, medication review, and treatment optimization planning.

patients.name: Chelsea Nelson
medications: Dupilumab 300 mg subcutaneous injection every 2 weeks

careEvents.title: Annual physical and asthma assessment
careEvents.description: Baseline asthma evaluation. Patient reports well-controlled asthma on current regimen. FEV1 88% predicted.

careEvents.title: Acute asthma exacerbation - ER visit
careEvents.description: Patient presented with acute dyspnea and wheezing during winter pollen season. Treated with nebulized albuterol and oral corticosteroids.

careEvents.title: Referral to pulmonology
careEvents.description: Referred for pulmonary function testing and optimization of asthma therapy following ER visit.

careEvents.title: Pulmonology consultation
careEvents.description: Initial consultation with pulmonologist. Recommended escalation to biologic therapy (Dupilumab) given ER exacerbation and seasonal pattern. Discussed environmental triggers and inhaler technique.

careEvents.title: Chest X-ray
careEvents.description: Baseline chest imaging ordered by pulmonologist. Results: No acute infiltrates. Normal heart size and mediastinum.

careEvents.title: Pulmonary function testing
careEvents.description: Spirometry and bronchodilator response testing performed. FEV1 82% predicted, FVC 91% predicted. Mild obstructive pattern with 12% improvement post-bronchodilator.

careEvents.title: Asthma care coordination follow-up
careEvents.description: 3-month follow-up post-Dupilumab initiation. Patient reports improved symptom control and reduced rescue inhaler use. Discussed action plan and environmental controls.

careEvents.title: Pulmonology follow-up and assessment
careEvents.description: Scheduled follow-up to assess response to Dupilumab therapy and seasonal symptom pattern. Repeat spirometry planned.

careEvents.title: Initial Hypothyroidism Diagnosis
careEvents.description: Patient presented with fatigue, weight gain, and cold intolerance. TSH elevated at 8.2 mIU/L. Levothyroxine initiated at 50 mcg daily.

careEvents.title: TSH and Free T4 Level Check
careEvents.description: Follow-up labs 4 weeks after starting levothyroxine. TSH: 6.1 mIU/L, Free T4: 0.8 ng/dL. Dose increased to 75 mcg daily.

careEvents.title: Follow-up Visit - Dose Titration
careEvents.description: Patient reports improved energy and reduced fatigue. Discussed medication timing and avoidance of calcium/iron supplements within 4 hours of levothyroxine. TSH trending down. Plan to recheck labs in 6 weeks.

careEvents.title: TSH and Free T4 Level Check
careEvents.description: TSH: 2.8 mIU/L, Free T4: 1.1 ng/dL. Levels now within target range. Levothyroxine dose maintained at 75 mcg daily.

careEvents.title: Routine Thyroid Management Visit
careEvents.description: Patient stable on current levothyroxine dose. Reported good symptom control. Discussed annual monitoring plan and importance of medication compliance.

careEvents.title: Annual TSH and Free T4 Panel
careEvents.description: Annual monitoring labs for hypothyroidism management. TSH: 2.5 mIU/L, Free T4: 1.15 ng/dL. Levels stable and well-controlled.

careEvents.title: Scheduled Follow-up Visit
careEvents.description: Quarterly follow-up with endocrinologist to monitor TSH levels and assess for any medication adjustments needed.

patients.name: Rachel Martinez
patients.primaryDiagnosis: Major Depressive Disorder, Moderate, In Treatment

careEvents.title: Initial Psychiatric Evaluation
careEvents.description: Patient presented with depressed mood, anhedonia, fatigue, and poor sleep for 3 months. Formal assessment confirmed Major Depressive Disorder diagnosis.

careEvents.title: Psychiatry Follow-up Visit
careEvents.description: Sertraline 100 mg daily initiated. Patient advised on medication timeline and side effects.

careEvents.title: Referral to Psychotherapy
careEvents.description: Patient referred to Dr. Lisa Rodriguez for cognitive behavioral therapy to complement pharmacological treatment.

careEvents.title: First Therapy Session
careEvents.description: Initial session with therapist focusing on cognitive patterns and behavioral activation strategies.

careEvents.title: Psychiatry Med Review
careEvents.description: Patient reported partial improvement with Sertraline. Bupropion 150 mg daily added to address residual fatigue and motivation.

careEvents.title: Lab Work: Metabolic Panel and Thyroid Function
careEvents.description: Routine labs ordered to monitor for medication side effects and rule out medical contributors to depressive symptoms.

careEvents.title: Psychiatry Follow-up and Med Optimization
careEvents.description: Patient showing significant improvement. Sertraline increased to 150 mg and Bupropion increased to 300 mg XL for sustained effect.

careEvents.title: Ongoing Therapy Session
careEvents.description: Continued CBT sessions focusing on relapse prevention and developing coping strategies.

careEvents.title: Three-Month Psychiatry Check-in
careEvents.description: Patient reports sustained improvement in mood, energy, and sleep quality. Current medication regimen well-tolerated.

careEvents.title: Ongoing Psychotherapy
careEvents.description: Weekly therapy sessions continuing to consolidate gains and address emerging life stressors.

careEvents.title: Maintenance Psychiatry Visit
careEvents.description: Routine follow-up visit to monitor medication effectiveness and discuss long-term treatment planning.

careEvents.title: Lab Work: Annual Metabolic Panel
careEvents.description: Annual labs to assess overall health and continue monitoring for any medication-related changes.

careEvents.title: Rheumatology Initial Assessment
careEvents.description: Initial comprehensive rheumatology evaluation; started on biologic therapy (Adalimumab) with concurrent methotrexate

careEvents.title: Baseline Labs - RA Panel
careEvents.description: ESR, CRP, RF, anti-CCP, CBC, comprehensive metabolic panel, liver function tests before biologic initiation

careEvents.title: First Adalimumab Infusion
careEvents.description: Patient education and self-injection training for adalimumab; first dose administered in clinic

careEvents.title: Methotrexate Monitoring Labs
careEvents.description: CBC, liver function tests, and renal function to monitor methotrexate tolerance

careEvents.title: Hand & Wrist X-rays
careEvents.description: Baseline radiographs of hands and wrists to assess joint damage and inflammation; bilateral views

careEvents.title: Rheumatology Follow-up - Response Assessment
careEvents.description: Assessment of clinical response to adalimumab and methotrexate; joint examination; medication tolerability review

careEvents.title: Quarterly RA Monitoring Labs
careEvents.description: ESR, CRP, CBC, comprehensive metabolic panel, and liver function tests; assessment of disease activity markers

careEvents.title: Rheumatology Follow-up - Low Disease Activity Assessment
careEvents.description: Patient achieving low disease activity state; discussed long-term management strategy; reviewed adalimumab continuation

careEvents.title: RA Monitoring Labs - Quarterly
careEvents.description: ESR, CRP, CBC, liver function tests, and renal function; ongoing surveillance on biologic therapy

patients.name: James Mitchell
patients.primaryDiagnosis: Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD) with home oxygen dependency
medications: Home oxygen therapy 2-3 L/min continuous, 4-5 L/min with exertion

careEvents.title: Pulmonology Clinic Visit
careEvents.description: Initial comprehensive pulmonary assessment. Baseline spirometry performed showing FEV1 28% predicted. Home oxygen needs confirmed at 2-3 L/min continuous.

careEvents.title: High-Resolution Chest CT
careEvents.description: HRCT chest with and without contrast to assess emphysema distribution and rule out other pulmonary pathology. Findings consistent with advanced centrilobular emphysema.

careEvents.title: Echocardiography
careEvents.description: Transthoracic echocardiogram to assess cardiac function and pulmonary pressures. Findings: mild-to-moderate pulmonary hypertension, RVSP estimated at 52 mmHg.

careEvents.title: Pulmonary Rehabilitation & Self-Management Education
careEvents.description: Enrollment in 8-week outpatient pulmonary rehabilitation program. Topics: oxygen management, inhalation technique, energy conservation, nutrition, and activity planning.

careEvents.title: Acute COPD Exacerbation - Hospitalization
careEvents.description: Emergency department admission for acute exacerbation triggered by upper respiratory infection. Treated with IV corticosteroids, broad-spectrum antibiotics, and nebulized bronchodilators. Hospital stay 4 days. Discharged on prednisone taper and azithromycin.
careEvents.provider: Dr. Marcus Chen / Hospital Medicine Team
careEvents.details: Admitted 2024-08-05, discharged 2024-08-09

careEvents.title: Post-Hospitalization Follow-up
careEvents.description: Comprehensive follow-up visit 6 weeks post-hospitalization. Assessed recovery, medication compliance, home oxygen usage, and respiratory status. Discussed exacerbation triggers and prevention strategies.

careEvents.title: Spirometry and Arterial Blood Gas
careEvents.description: Quarterly pulmonary function testing and ABG to assess baseline status and oxygenation. Pending follow-up with pulmonology.

careEvents.title: Routine Pulmonology Follow-up
careEvents.description: Quarterly clinic visit with Dr. Chen to review spirometry results, adjust oxygen prescription if needed, and optimize medication regimen.

careEvents.title: Initial Nephrology Consultation
careEvents.description: Established care with nephrology for CKD management. Baseline eGFR 38 mL/min/1.73m². Discussed progression risk and medication optimization.

careEvents.title: Comprehensive Metabolic Panel & Lipid Panel
careEvents.description: Baseline labs: Creatinine 1.9, eGFR 38, K+ 5.1, Phosphorus 3.8, Hemoglobin 10.2 g/dL

careEvents.title: Renal Ultrasound
careEvents.description: Bilateral kidneys normal size without hydronephrosis or masses. Increased echogenicity consistent with CKD.

careEvents.title: Cardiology Evaluation
careEvents.description: Risk stratification for CKD patient with prior MI. EKG normal. Continued current cardioprotective regimen.

careEvents.title: Nutritionist Referral for Renal Diet
careEvents.description: Referred to registered dietitian for phosphorus/potassium/sodium restriction education. CKD Stage 3b dietary management.

careEvents.title: CKD Disease Management Class
careEvents.description: Attended 2-hour education session on CKD progression, medication adherence, dietary modifications, and symptom recognition.

careEvents.title: Quarterly Nephrology Labs
careEvents.description: Creatinine 2.0, eGFR 37, K+ 5.3, Phosphorus 4.1. Mild decline noted. Medication adjustment planned.

careEvents.title: Nephrology Follow-up Visit
careEvents.description: Reviewed declining renal function. Adjusted sodium bicarbonate dose. Discussed SGLT2 inhibitor trial eligibility pending cardiology clearance.

careEvents.title: Quarterly Labs & Urinalysis
careEvents.description: eGFR 36, creatinine 2.05, albumin-to-creatinine ratio 420 mg/g. Persistent albuminuria.

careEvents.title: Chest X-ray
careEvents.description: Ordered for baseline assessment given CKD/CAD overlap. No acute cardiopulmonary process.

careEvents.title: Cardiology Reassessment
careEvents.description: Annual reassessment. Clearance given for SGLT2 inhibitor initiation. BP goal <130/80 achieved.

careEvents.title: Mid-year Nephrology Panel
careEvents.description: eGFR 34, creatinine 2.2, phosphorus 4.4, K+ 5.4. Trending toward Stage 4. SGLT2i started.

careEvents.title: Nephrology Follow-up Post SGLT2i Initiation
careEvents.description: Tolerating empagliflozin 10 mg well. No adverse effects. Recheck labs in 2 months.

careEvents.title: Quarterly Labs - Overdue
careEvents.description: Patient delayed scheduling. Labs now 3 weeks overdue. Critical for CKD trajectory assessment.
careEvents.details: Patient reported busy schedule; care coordinator following up to schedule ASAP to avoid treatment gap

careEvents.title: Primary Care Annual Physical
careEvents.description: Comprehensive assessment with PCP. Review medication compliance, diabetes control, BP trends. Scheduled labs pending.

careEvents.title: Nephrology Q3 Month Follow-up
careEvents.description: Assessment of SGLT2i response, kidney function trend, anemia management, and progression to Stage 4 risk. Anticipate possible GLP-1 agonist consideration.

careEvents.title: Social Work Consultation - Upcoming
careEvents.description: Referral for advanced care planning discussion, disability evaluation, and financial resources for ongoing care.

patients.name: Jacob Parsons
patients.primaryDiagnosis: Heart Failure with Reduced Ejection Fraction (HFrEF), NYHA Class III
conditions: Coronary artery disease s/p prior MI (2019)

careEvents.title: Cardiology Initial Consultation
careEvents.description: First evaluation for worsening dyspnea and fatigue; echocardiogram ordered

careEvents.title: Transthoracic Echocardiogram
careEvents.description: EF 28%, severe LV dilatation, moderate MR, EDD 62 mm

careEvents.title: Hospital Admission - Acute Decompensated Heart Failure
careEvents.description: 3-day admission for acute HF exacerbation; IV diuretics and inotropic support initiated; discharged on optimized regimen
careEvents.provider: Dr. Patricia Rodriguez & Dr. Marcus Chen

careEvents.title: Cardiology Follow-up Post-Discharge
careEvents.description: Volume status reassessed, medication compliance reviewed; ICD evaluation recommended

careEvents.title: Cardiac MRI for ICD Candidacy
careEvents.description: Confirmed EF 28%, extensive LV scarring in anterior wall consistent with prior MI

careEvents.title: Implantable Cardioverter-Defibrillator (ICD) Implantation
careEvents.description: Single-chamber ICD placed for primary prevention; post-operative course uncomplicated

careEvents.title: ICD Follow-up and Device Check
careEvents.description: ICD interrogation normal; no arrhythmias detected; incision healing well

careEvents.title: Cardiac Rehabilitation Program Enrollment
careEvents.description: 12-week supervised exercise and education program initiated post-ICD placement

careEvents.title: Hospital Admission - Recurrent HF Decompensation
careEvents.description: 4-day admission for volume overload; IV furosemide escalated; Digoxin started; reviewed medication adherence issues
careEvents.provider: Dr. Patricia Rodriguez & Dr. Marcus Chen

careEvents.title: Nephrology Consultation for CKD
careEvents.description: Creatinine 1.9, eGFR 32; discussed SGLT2i continuation and renal function monitoring

careEvents.title: Routine Labs and BNP
careEvents.description: Overdue labs ordered; BNP 850 (elevated), K+ 5.1 (high normal), Cr 2.0 (slight increase)
careEvents.details: Patient reports difficulty getting to lab; labs needed for 3 weeks

careEvents.title: Cardiology Follow-up Exam
careEvents.description: Upcoming routine HF check; medication review, volume status assessment, ICD check planned

patients.name: Margaret Susan Chen
conditions: Metastatic adenocarcinoma of breast to lungs and liver
medications: Palliative oxycodone 5-10 mg PO Q4-6 hours as needed
Ondansetron 8 mg PO Q8 hours as needed

careEvents.title: Oncology Consultation - Diagnosis & Treatment Planning
careEvents.description: Initial presentation with biopsy-confirmed metastatic breast cancer. Imaging revealed pulmonary and hepatic metastases. Multidisciplinary tumor board review completed. Palliative chemotherapy regimen initiated.

careEvents.title: Port-a-Cath Placement
careEvents.description: Successful placement of central venous access device for chemotherapy infusion.

careEvents.title: Baseline Laboratory Panel & Tumor Markers
careEvents.description: Complete metabolic panel, CBC, HER2 receptor status (positive), CEA and CA 15-3 tumor markers drawn. Results obtained to establish baseline for chemotherapy monitoring.
careEvents.provider: Quest Diagnostics, supervised by Dr. Valdez

careEvents.title: Baseline CT Chest/Abdomen/Pelvis with IV Contrast
careEvents.description: Cross-sectional imaging demonstrating bilateral pulmonary nodules (largest 2.8 cm right lower lobe) and two hepatic lesions consistent with metastatic disease. RECIST baseline established.

careEvents.title: Cycle 1 Chemotherapy Infusion (Paclitaxel/Carboplatin/Herceptin)
careEvents.description: First cycle of combination chemotherapy administered. Pre-medications given. Tolerating infusions without acute adverse events.
careEvents.provider: Infusion Center, Sarah Martinez RN, OCN

careEvents.title: Oncology Follow-up & Adverse Event Assessment
careEvents.description: Day 21 post-cycle 1. Mild nausea controlled with antiemetics. Grade 1 fatigue reported. CBC shows adequate counts. Cycle 2 approved to proceed.

careEvents.title: Cycle 2 Chemotherapy Infusion
careEvents.description: Second cycle of paclitaxel/carboplatin/herceptin completed. Patient reports improved nausea management. Developing mild peripheral neuropathy in feet.
careEvents.provider: Infusion Center, Sarah Martinez RN, OCN

careEvents.title: Monitoring Laboratory Panel - Post Cycle 3
careEvents.description: CBC, CMP, HER2 status confirmation. WBC 3.2 (low-normal), Hemoglobin 10.1 (mild anemia), Platelets 145 (acceptable). Albumin 3.4 (slightly low, nutritional support recommended).

careEvents.title: Response Assessment CT Chest/Abdomen/Pelvis
careEvents.description: Post-3-cycle imaging showing partial response: right lower lobe pulmonary nodule reduced to 1.9 cm; hepatic lesions show 15-20% size reduction. RECIST partial response documented.

careEvents.title: Palliative Care Consultation
careEvents.description: Referral made for supportive care, symptom management optimization, advance care planning discussion, and psychological support coordination.

careEvents.title: Palliative Care Initial Visit & Advance Directives
careEvents.description: Comprehensive symptom assessment performed. Pain regimen optimized. Goals of care discussion held with patient and family. Advance directive documentation initiated. Psychologist referral placed.

careEvents.title: Chemotherapy Side Effects & Coping Strategies Session
careEvents.description: Educational session covering neuropathy management, cognitive impairment ('chemo brain') strategies, nutrition optimization, and fatigue management techniques.
careEvents.provider: Sarah Martinez RN, OCN with Dr. Michele Wu, PhD

careEvents.title: MRI Brain with Contrast (Rule Out CNS Metastases)
careEvents.description: Screening for intracranial involvement given advanced disease. No acute abnormality detected. No evidence of brain metastases.

careEvents.title: Routine Monitoring Labs (Overdue)
careEvents.description: Quarterly CBC, CMP, and tumor marker assessment overdue per protocol. Patient reports ongoing fatigue and intermittent nausea affecting compliance.
careEvents.details: Patient has deferred labs due to transportation challenges and increased symptom burden. Care coordinator assisting with scheduling.

careEvents.title: Oncology Restaging & Treatment Plan Adjustment
careEvents.description: Clinical reassessment after 11+ months of active treatment. Imaging to determine continued response vs. progression. Potential for treatment modification or escalation based on imaging findings.

careEvents.title: Fasting Labs
careEvents.description: HbA1c, fasting glucose, comprehensive metabolic panel, lipid panel
careEvents.details: HbA1c: 9.2% (above goal of <7%), Fasting glucose: 187 mg/dL

careEvents.title: Primary Care Visit
careEvents.description: Follow-up for diabetes management and medication review

careEvents.title: Endocrinology Consultation
careEvents.description: Comprehensive diabetes assessment, medication adjustment, insulin titration plan
careEvents.details: Increased Glimepiride to 4 mg daily; insulin increased from 30 to 40 units nightly; scheduled follow-up labs in 6 weeks

careEvents.title: Diabetes Education Class
careEvents.description: Group session on carbohydrate counting, blood glucose monitoring, and recognizing hypoglycemia

careEvents.title: Ophthalmology - Diabetic Eye Exam
careEvents.description: Dilated eye exam and fundus photography; assessment of retinopathy progression
careEvents.details: Confirmed non-proliferative diabetic retinopathy; mild microaneurysms noted; no immediate laser treatment needed; advised tight glucose control

careEvents.title: Repeat Fasting Labs
careEvents.description: HbA1c, fasting glucose, comprehensive metabolic panel
careEvents.details: HbA1c: 8.8% (slight improvement), Fasting glucose: 165 mg/dL

careEvents.title: Podiatry Foot Assessment
careEvents.description: Comprehensive foot exam, monofilament testing, assessment of neuropathic pain and callus formation
careEvents.details: Confirmed moderate peripheral neuropathy; protective sensation reduced; no active ulcers; prescribed custom insoles; advised on daily foot checks

careEvents.title: Individual Nutrition Counseling
careEvents.description: One-on-one consultation on meal planning, portion control, and weight management

careEvents.title: Endocrinology Follow-up
careEvents.description: Review of home glucose logs, medication tolerance assessment, adjustment of diabetes management plan
careEvents.details: Reviewed logs showing dawn phenomenon; morning glucose 150-180 mg/dL; discussed potential for additional basal insulin

careEvents.title: Comprehensive Labs (Overdue)
careEvents.description: HbA1c, fasting glucose, comprehensive metabolic panel, lipid panel, urine microalbumin
careEvents.details: Originally scheduled for 2024-05-01; patient missed initial appointment; requires completion for endocrinology follow-up planning

careEvents.title: Referral to Endocrinology (Upcoming)
careEvents.description: Urgent follow-up with endocrinologist to address persistently elevated HbA1c and neuropathy progression
careEvents.details: Scheduled following missed labs; address medication compliance and consider insulin pump therapy

careEvents.title: Retinal Imaging and Assessment (Upcoming)
careEvents.description: Repeat dilated eye exam and optical coherence tomography (OCT) to monitor retinopathy progression

careEvents.title: Initial Pre-diabetes Screening
careEvents.description: Patient presented with fatigue and increased thirst. Fasting glucose 118 mg/dL. Pre-diabetes diagnosis confirmed. Discussed lifestyle modifications and medication initiation.

careEvents.title: Hemoglobin A1C Test
careEvents.description: Baseline A1C drawn at primary care office.

careEvents.title: Referral to Endocrinology
careEvents.description: Referred to Dr. James Patel for specialized diabetes management and treatment optimization. Patient indicated to schedule within 4-6 weeks.
careEvents.details: Appointment not scheduled. Patient reported schedule conflicts and transportation barriers. Follow-up attempts by care coordinator on 2024-10-22 and 2024-11-05 unsuccessful.

careEvents.title: Diabetes Prevention Program Education
careEvents.description: Group education session on diet, exercise, and glucose monitoring. Patient attended; provided with educational materials and glucose log.

careEvents.title: Repeat Hemoglobin A1C and Metabolic Panel
careEvents.description: Follow-up labs ordered at 3-month interval to assess response to treatment and lifestyle changes.
careEvents.details: Labs ordered but patient has not completed. Lab requisition expires 2024-11-15. No glucose values logged in past 6 weeks. Medication refills pending lab results.

careEvents.title: Pre-diabetes Follow-up Visit - Urgent
careEvents.description: Appointment scheduled to reassess metabolic status, review overdue lab work, address care gaps, and determine need for intensified intervention or endocrinology referral.

careEvents.title: Annual Physical Exam
careEvents.description: Patient presented for routine annual physical. Reviewed cardiovascular risk factors given hyperlipidemia diagnosis. Discussed need for updated lipid panel and colonoscopy screening.

careEvents.title: Lipid Panel
careEvents.description: Fasting lipid panel completed. Total cholesterol 245 mg/dL, LDL 160 mg/dL, HDL 38 mg/dL, Triglycerides 185 mg/dL. Results indicate suboptimal control on current statin therapy.

careEvents.title: Cardiology Consultation
careEvents.description: Referral follow-up with cardiology regarding lipid management. Discussed potential need for additional lipid-lowering therapy. Patient agreed to diet and exercise modifications before medication adjustment.

careEvents.title: Lipid Panel - OVERDUE
careEvents.description: Lipid panel was due 9 months post-initial labs. Patient has not completed despite multiple reminders. Critical for monitoring statin efficacy and cardiovascular risk.
careEvents.details: Originally scheduled for March 2024. Patient reported busy with work and has been postponing. Reminder sent via patient portal and phone.

careEvents.title: Colonoscopy Screening - OVERDUE
careEvents.description: Screening colonoscopy was recommended during 2023 annual physical as patient is 58 years old and due for routine colorectal cancer screening. Procedure order placed but patient has not scheduled.
careEvents.details: Order placed June 2023. Patient has declined twice citing scheduling conflicts and anxiety about procedure. Care coordinator has attempted contact multiple times without response.

careEvents.title: Follow-up Visit - Care Gap Addressed
careEvents.description: Patient came in for medication refill. Care coordinator reviewed overdue lipid panel and colonoscopy. Patient acknowledged need for screening but requested to schedule colonoscopy in January 2025. Offered referral to colonoscopy prep education.

careEvents.title: Lipid Panel - IN PROGRESS
careEvents.description: Lipid panel ordered and patient scheduled for December 2024. Lab requisition provided. Fasting labs to assess response to current Atorvastatin therapy after 18 months.
careEvents.details: Patient committed to completing labs this month prior to year-end visit with cardiologist.

careEvents.title: Colonoscopy Screening - Upcoming
careEvents.description: Routine colorectal cancer screening scheduled. Pre-procedure visit and prep instructions to be sent to patient. Given age 58, screening is overdue and clinically indicated.

patients.name: Jessica Martinez
patients.primaryDiagnosis: Postpartum depression with missed follow-up care

careEvents.title: Hospital Discharge - Postpartum
careEvents.description: Patient discharged after uncomplicated vaginal delivery. Baby born 6/13/2024. Postpartum vital signs stable. Counseled on postpartum warning signs and follow-up care.

careEvents.title: 6-Week Postpartum Check-up
careEvents.description: Routine postpartum evaluation. Patient reports fatigue and mild mood changes. Hemoglobin 9.2 g/dL (anemia). Vital signs normal. Started iron supplementation.

careEvents.title: Postpartum Mental Health Screening - Scheduled
careEvents.description: Patient scheduled for postpartum depression screening (PHQ-9) and initial psychiatric evaluation. Appointment time provided: 2:00 PM at Behavioral Health Center.
careEvents.details: Patient missed appointment on 2024-07-15. No-show reported. Rescheduling attempts unsuccessful - no response to phone calls or messages.

careEvents.title: 12-Week Postpartum Visit (Rescheduled)
careEvents.description: Follow-up postpartum examination to assess physical recovery, wound healing, and mood screening.
careEvents.details: Scheduled for 2024-08-30 but patient did not attend. Care coordinator attempted contact on 3 occasions. Patient reported transportation barriers. Now 3+ months overdue.

careEvents.title: Repeat Hemoglobin Lab Work
careEvents.description: Follow-up anemia assessment. Lab order placed to evaluate response to iron supplementation.
careEvents.details: Lab requisition provided at 6-week visit but specimen never collected. Patient reports difficulty scheduling appointment at lab due to childcare.

careEvents.title: Mental Health Intake and PHQ-9 Assessment
careEvents.description: Comprehensive psychiatric evaluation including postpartum depression screening, medication management discussion, and psychotherapy planning.
careEvents.details: Recently rescheduled by care coordinator. Patient confirmed attendance. Telehealth option offered to reduce barriers.

careEvents.title: Primary Care Annual Exam
careEvents.description: Annual physical examination. BP 148/92 mmHg. Patient reports inconsistent medication adherence due to cost and side effects. Discussed importance of blood pressure control.

careEvents.title: Lipid Panel
careEvents.description: Fasting lipid panel ordered to assess cholesterol levels given family history of early MI.

careEvents.title: Cardiology Consultation
careEvents.description: Referred by PCP due to uncontrolled hypertension and family history of cardiac disease. EKG performed, echo recommended for further evaluation.

careEvents.title: Transthoracic Echocardiogram
careEvents.description: Cardiac ultrasound to assess left ventricular function and rule out hypertensive cardiomyopathy.
careEvents.details: Results pending final cardiology interpretation

careEvents.title: Hemoglobin A1C and Metabolic Panel - OVERDUE
careEvents.description: Labs ordered to monitor diabetes control and kidney function on current medications.
careEvents.details: Patient missed lab appointment on 2024-06-15; rescheduled twice, has not completed. Last A1C was 8.2% in February 2024.

careEvents.title: Follow-up with PCP - OVERDUE
careEvents.description: Scheduled follow-up to address medication adherence, review cardiology results, and reassess blood pressure control.
careEvents.details: Patient did not attend. Called twice by clinic staff but was unreachable. Blood pressure continues uncontrolled per patient self-monitoring at home (145-155 systolic).

careEvents.title: Nutrition and Lifestyle Counseling - Upcoming
careEvents.description: Scheduled dietary counseling to address diet compliance, sodium restriction, and weight management as part of care coordination for hypertension and diabetes management.

careEvents.title: Cardiology Follow-up - Upcoming
careEvents.description: Follow-up visit with cardiology to discuss echo results, medication optimization, and risk stratification.
